summary: Tested Phase 1 of GAIA v1.7 (Saved Charts + Settings) — frontend only. All 7 requested scenarios PASS functionally: (1) Home top-bar has GAIA brand, LangToggle, and new nav-settings cog; (2) Settings page renders with lang/hemi segments, 0-chart counter, disabled Clear button, and Version 1.7.0; (3) Saved-charts empty state shows BookOpen icon, message, and empty-cta-create that navigates to /chart; (4) Full save flow works — submit chart → result → open-save-chart → modal → Marie/Sister inputs → save-modal-confirm → chart-already-saved pill appears → /saved-charts lists 'Marie' with 'Sister' note and elements chips → open-saved-<id> re-opens /result; (5) Persistence across browser reload confirmed — 'Marie' still present, settings shows '1 chart stored'; (6) FR translation persists across reload (heading switches to 'Chartes sauvegardées', 'Aucune charte sauvegardée' empty state, French CTA 'GÉNÉRER MA CHARTE TERRESTRE'); (7) Delete flow works with inline web confirm (delete-saved-<id> → confirm-delete-<id> removes card → empty state returns). Bilingual + AsyncStorage persistence via Zustand persist middleware is functioning correctly. No backend was touched and none was tested.

issues: Console warning repeated on every screen: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Likely originates from react-native-web compatibility in a child component (e.g. StarryBackground, Animated.View, or lucide-react-native). Not blocking but should be tracked.

action_items: Optional: investigate the 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' console warning emitted on every screen — likely from a third-party RN-Web component; consider migrating any explicit pointerEvents= props into style={{ pointerEvents: ... }}.

Optional polish: when the Clear-all button is disabled on web, the rendered TouchableOpacity does not set the HTML 'disabled' attribute on its underlying element (Playwright is_disabled() returns False even though disabled={chartsCount===0} prevents onPress). The visual opacity-0.4 cue and the onPress guard work; no functional impact, but consider rendering with accessibilityState={{ disabled: chartsCount===0 }} so screen readers and tests pick it up.
critical_code_review_comments: settings.tsx and saved-charts.tsx both correctly use Platform.OS === 'web' to fall back from native Alert to inline confirm UI — good cross-platform handling.
result.tsx: findDuplicate is recomputed in a useEffect on every chart/birthHour change — clean, idempotent. saveChart returns the id and the UI swaps to chart-already-saved testID — verified.
saved-charts.tsx: card rendering uses entry.chart?.sign?.[lang] with a graceful '—' fallback; elements chips render only when array non-empty. Good defensive coding.
All new screens use SafeAreaView with edges={['top']} and StyleSheet.create — adheres to RN best practices.

Zustand persist middleware is wired correctly to AsyncStorage (AsyncStorage on RN, localStorage shim on web via AsyncStorage from @react-native-async-storage/async-storage). Persistence confirmed across hard reload.
No deprecated expo packages detected in the new files (no expo-av / expo-barcode-scanner / expo-background-fetch usage).
seed_data_creation: Test created and then deleted a single saved chart with label='Marie', notes='Sister' via the UI. Final state: 0 saved charts (cleaned up by delete flow in Step 7). No residual seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 1 of GAIA v1.7 (Saved Charts library + Settings screen) is fully functional on the Expo web preview at http://localhost:3000. All testIDs from the request spec are present and wired correctly. AsyncStorage persistence verified across hard reload. Next phases (Stripe/Weave/multi-person) are not implemented yet and were intentionally skipped per the review request.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no functional issues found. Note for future test runs: the new screens use react-native-reanimated FadeInDown with delays (80–290ms). is_visible() Playwright probes immediately after navigation can return False during the entering animation; add page.wait_for_timeout(500–800) after navigating to /settings or /saved-charts before reading element states.
